title: Patch for Windows Defender 0-day could allow attackers to fill hard disk
gist: Microsoft's patch for a Windows Defender zero-day (RoguePlanet, CVE-2026-50656) allegedly introduced a new disk-exhaustion flaw, amid an escalating disclosure feud between Microsoft and the researcher NightmareEclipse.

themes.theme: The patch that created a new flaw

insights.claim: Microsoft's patch for the RoguePlanet zero-day (CVE-2026-50656) introduced defense-in-depth mitigations that can leak 8 bytes when opening a file and allow attackers to exhaust all available disk space.
insights.speaker: Dan Goodin
insights.so_what: A patch that opens a new attack path underscores Microsoft's QA gaps in its security engine, a reputational and enterprise-risk concern.

insights.claim: The original RoguePlanet flaw let remote attackers gain administrative control of Windows 10 and 11 machines even when real-time protection was disabled.
insights.speaker: Dan Goodin
insights.so_what: A remote admin-control flaw in the default AV engine affects the entire Windows install base, a systemic exposure.

insights.claim: The new bug is exploitable via a custom SMB server serving a malicious file followed by a massive alternate data stream, causing Defender to hang and lock the disk; it won't crash the machine but makes Windows misbehave with a full disk.
insights.speaker: Dan Goodin
insights.so_what: A denial-of-service via SMB and ADS is a practical enterprise-network risk worth patch-management attention.
insights.locator: NightmareEclipse said that a malicious actor

insights.claim: NightmareEclipse and Microsoft have been feuding since May over a silently patched vulnerability, prompting the researcher to release exploit code before patches and Microsoft to make a veiled legal threat before relenting after backlash.
insights.speaker: Dan Goodin
insights.so_what: A breakdown in responsible disclosure means more unpatched zero-days going public, raising near-term exploitation risk for Windows users.
insights.locator: NightmareEclipse and Microsoft have been locked in a heated dispute
